subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 27 Jun
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 27 Jun.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find SIP weekly update below for your review, including follow-up
questions from last weekly on 1) CS; 2) Taobao (in pdf).

Performance Dashboard (as of 25 Jun)

A/ Topline/P&L

* Taobao program Jul target to be aligned

Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Jun proj ADO 230k, 104% RR, %CB +0.3pp MoM; ADG $2.4M, 104%RR,
   %CB +0.2pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP ADO 105% RR, %CB +0.3pp MoM; higher RR driven by higher new
      ads ramp up with BPSF budget support and IDSIP fraud clawback.
ADG and ADG%
      slightly missed targets due to ABS lower than expected driven by 1)
      platform MP PRM increases significantly. 2) Platform provides SIP
      additional item rebates in SG/MY to drive ADO
      2.

      CNSIP ADO 103% RR, %CB stable MoM; RR on target driven by ongoing
      optimization initiatives (e.g., ads markup removal for
non-performing SKUs)
      2.

   [P&L] Jun proj combined PC2 $3.3M, $69k under-performance
   1.

      Local SIP $159k out-performance, mainly due to higher SLS profit as
      vol increase led to lower cost
      2.

      CN SIP $228k under-performance, mainly due to reinvesting 5% GMV to
      counter revenue loss from mgt fee removal, to protect topline; will
      maintain reinvestment in Jun-Aug


B/ CNSIP revamp progress update

   1.

   New seller pitching/rejected seller repitching post management fee
   removal (May+Jun batch): total 27k GPs pitched; overall 65% pitching
   success rate on GP level and 39% pitching success rate on SKU level (lower
   than 70% target)


   2.

   Top rejection reasons are: 1) worry about potential loss-making (40% sku
   coverage); 2) want to focus on direct shop (30% sku coverage); 3) not
   familiar with SIP model (11% sku coverage)
   3.

   Next steps to improve pitching success
   1.

      Before next repitch, add a CNSC banner + improve mass pitching comms
      to educate seller further on how settlement price

content.answer:
New seller pitching/rejected seller repitching post management fee
   removal (May+Jun batch): total 27k GPs pitched; overall 65% pitching
   success rate on GP level and 39% pitching success rate on SKU level (lower
   than 70% target)



For the 17k GPs who have accepted, what are the next steps?


   - We will complete their shop opening and SKU listing by 2 Jul, and
   start to sell on 3 Jul. SIP will invest budgets from the new shop
   commission waiver to invest on new SKU traffic boosting



D/ CS (as of 22 Jun 2025)





Waiting time in queue still feels long in general for ID lanes? And MYSG?


   - ID lanes: because ID experienced a spike in chat volume last Monday,
   they are still waiting for manager approval to increase HC. The request is
   expected to be approved within this week. L7D average waiting time has
   improved to 6.1 minutes.
   - MYSG: because SG lanes faced a manpower shortage due to the
   resignation of agents. New agents have been hired and will be ready to
   handle live chats from 30th June after one week of training. Improvement in
   waiting time should be seen from this week onwards.



Performance (IDMY, VNMY) - As of 28 June



Highlights

MYSG: topline on track: MTD ADO 7.8k, 110% RR, +7% MoM; SKU count 493k,
   109% RR; # sellers behind target mainly due to slow onboarding process
   of newly acquired sellers, May-MTD 917 new sellers acquired, 492 (54%)
   in onboarding process; 151 (16%) shop opened but in HM to finalize pricing;
   274 (30%) active selling. Product solution to support fast onboarding
   expected in early/mid-Aug

IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24 Jun; 6
   starts to sell

VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun; 105
   start to sell, MTD ADO 2



For 2, seems very few sellers?

   -

   Pitching pool batch 1 of 1.3K target managed sellers kicked off pitching
   from 28 May; out of which, 103 accepted, 209 rejected, and 1K pending
   responses
   -

   Pitching progress is slower than expected because 1) IDSIP/Direct SMT
   team is focusing on GR event preparation and acquired seller onboarding; 2)
   local RM takes longer time to revert seller feedback than expected
   -

   Next steps: 1) RMs will drive for definite answers by 4 July; 2) have
   expanded pitching pool to additional 1.4K managed sellers as of 30 June,
   target to conclude this pool by 11 July



For 2 and 3, how are we ensuring that they succeed? Do they partake in
local programs right now?

   -

   Growth package designed to support direct seller in the first 3 months
   with 1) commission fee waivers up to 500 orders per month 2) free ads
   credit with 200RM per shop 3) ads voucher with 50% off, cap at $200 RM /
   month 4) 5 CFS slots per seller per month
   -

   Checking with product team on what marketing tools sellers can use and
   participation process, to prioritise MY spike day LTD and voucher enrollment

content.question: MYSG: topline on track: MTD ADO 7.8k, 110% RR, +7% MoM; SKU count
IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24
VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun;

content.answer:
MYSG: topline on track: MTD ADO 7.8k, 110% RR, +7% MoM; SKU count
493k, 109% RR; # sellers behind target mainly due to slow onboarding
process of newly acquired sellers, May-MTD 917 new sellers acquired, 492
(54%) in onboarding process; 151 (16%) shop opened but in HM to finalize
pricing; 274 (30%) active selling. Product solution to support fast
onboarding expected in early/mid-Aug

IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24
Jun; 6 starts to sell

VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun;
105 start to sell, MTD ADO 2



For 2, seems very few sellers?

   -

   Pitching pool batch 1 of 1.3K target managed sellers kicked off pitching
   from 28 May; out of which, 103 accepted, 209 rejected, and 1K pending
   responses



Any reason why so many reject?




   -

   83% rejected sellers “want to focus on local businesses” or have
   “manpower limitations”. We suggest to park them for now and repitch next
   year when they are more ready to expand oversea business
   -

   2% “already has MY store” - not to repitch as the SKU supply is already
   available to MY
   -

   14% has concerns on Direct selling ops such as “high admin fee (i.e.
   platform fee)”, “export limitations”, “worried about export success” - RMs
   will repitch this group in Jul after validating limitations, to educate
   them how to hide admin fee in listing price, etc.



For 2 and 3, how are we ensuring that they succeed? Do they partake in
local programs right now?

   -

   Growth package designed to support direct seller in the first 3 months
   with 1) commission fee waivers up to 500 orders per month 2) free ads
   credit with 200RM per shop 3) ads voucher with 50% off, cap at $200 RM /
   month 4) 5 CFS slots per seller per month
   -

   Checking with product team on what marketing tools sellers can use and
   participation process, to prioritise MY spike day LTD and voucher enrolment



Are we making it easy enough for sellers now? For eg. we should just give
more at the start to grow them. As compared to asking for co-fund etc. and
hard to move forward



Probably easier to ask at later stage when proven orders

   -

   Yes, the current growth package does not ask any seller co-investment
   commitment in the first 3 months; it serves to encourage sellers to test
   out various marketing tools in each import market (e.g., ads, CFS)

content.question: MYSG: topline on track: MTD ADO 7.8k, 110% RR, +7% MoM; SKU count
IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24
VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun;

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see our responses in blue.

Slide 4

   -

   For new to Shopee direct sellers, seems like impact to SKUs and ADO is
   very low? Refering to pure incremental numbers
   -

      By Dec '25, we target to have 3.8k incremental ADO from newly
      acquired sellers in Fashion, ~19% of Fashion total incremental ADO;
      and 0.3k incremental ADO from new sellers in Furniture, ~24% of
      Furniture total incremental ADO.
      -

      In Jun '25, the contribution is still low as it is early in the ramp
      up phase. ADO will gradually increase as SKU efficiency increases,
      supported by new seller program incentives on traffic investments &
      commission waiver as sellers meet commitment on SKU upload.
      -

         To illustrate the growth, a new Fashion seller onboarded in Apr is
         projected to grow from Jun (M3) 23, to 72 ADO (3.2x in Dec),
similar for
         Furniture: Jun (M3) 2 to 18 ADO (9x in Dec), high ASP
products usually need
         longer ramp-up period



Slide 5

   -

   For the 5 sellers who have opened shop, is their full SKU list in? And
   is pricing ok? I think we need to handhold the initial batch to be
   resounding successes..
   -

   We need to probably check SLA as well whether these sellers ship out as
   we go along
   -

      Yes, their full SKU list is in, with a total of 26k SKUs. However,
      their price competitiveness isn’t good, with SHP prices being on average
      ~50% higher than TB. We’ll plan to fix this by (1) Short-term:
To work with
      sellers to lower price for top SKUs (ETA; to: start next week) (2)
      Mid-term: ERPs to build a mass discount function, and work with
sellers to
      lower their price ETA end-Jul
      -

      Concurrently, we’re working with BI to set up daily tracking
      monitoring topline and ops metrics i.e. ship out time, NFRs, etc




Slide 6

   -

   How many sellers from JST and W

content.question:
Hi Ian,



Please see our responses in blue.



Slide 4

   - For new to Shopee direct sellers, seems like impact to SKUs and ADO is
   very low?

Refering to pure incremental numbers


   - By Dec '25, we target to have 3.8k incremental ADO from newly acquired
      sellers in Fashion, *~19%* of Fashion total incremental ADO; and 0.3k
      incremental ADO from new sellers in Furniture, *~24%* of Furniture
      total incremental ADO.
      - In Jun '25, the contribution is still low as it is early in the
      ramp up phase. ADO will gradually increase as SKU efficiency increases,
      supported by new seller program incentives on traffic investments &
      commission waiver as sellers meet commitment on SKU upload.


   - To illustrate the growth, a new Fashion seller onboarded in Apr is
         projected to grow from Jun (M3) 23, to 72 ADO (3.2x in Dec),
similar for
         Furniture: Jun (M3) 2 to 18 ADO (9x in Dec), high ASP
products usually need
         longer ramp-up period



This is still low in general from an impact point of view



Slide 5

   - For the 5 sellers who have opened shop, is their full SKU list in?

And
   is pricing ok?

I think we need to handhold the initial batch to be
   resounding successes..
   - We need to probably check SLA as well whether these sellers ship out
   as we go along


   - Yes, their full SKU list is in, with a total of 26k SKUs. However,
      their price competitiveness isn’t good, with SHP prices being on average
      ~50% higher than TB. We’ll plan to fix this by (1) Short-term:
To work with
      sellers to lower price for top SKUs (ETA; to: start next week) (2)
      Mid-term: ERPs to build a mass discount function, and work with
sellers to
      lower their price ETA end-Jul
      - Concurrently, we’re working with BI to set up daily tracking
      monitoring topline and ops metrics i.e. ship out time, NFRs, etc



Why is the pricing so bad to begin with?

Slide 8

   - WDT still feels slower in overall progression?

Anything we need to do
   there?

- Integration with WDT started 3 weeks later than JST, hence progress
      lags JST
      - However, they’re similarly supportive to fix the issues, and we’ll
      have another discussion with them tomorrow to confirm if they can fix all
      issues by end-Jul

What is the outcome here?

Slide 18  and 22

   - In general just not very good, any sense why?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,



Please see our responses in purple.




Slide 5

   -

   For the 5 sellers who have opened shop, is their full SKU list in? And
   is pricing ok? I think we need to handhold the initial batch to be
   resounding successes..
   -

   We need to probably check SLA as well whether these sellers ship out as
   we go along


   -

   Yes, their full SKU list is in, with a total of 26k SKUs. However, their
   price competitiveness isn’t good, with SHP prices being on average ~50%
   higher than TB. We’ll plan to fix this by (1) Short-term: To work with
   sellers to lower price for top SKUs (ETA; to: start next week) (2)
   Mid-term: ERPs to build a mass discount function, and work with sellers to
   lower their price ETA end-Jul
   -

   Concurrently, we’re working with BI to set up daily tracking monitoring
   topline and ops metrics i.e. ship out time, NFRs, etcv

Why is the pricing so bad to begin with?



   -

   The price that JST/WDT stores is the 一口价 which is not the discounted
   price that is offered on TB platforms. Hence, there’s a price discrepancy
   between TB and SHP.
   -

   The way for us to fix this is to work with ERPs for a mass discount
   feature that sellers can set discounts for SHP at scale on their ERP, ETA
   for feature TBC.







Slide 8

   -

   WDT still feels slower in overall progression? Anything we need to do
   there?


   -

   Integration with WDT started 3 weeks later than JST, hence progress lags
   JST
   -

   However, they’re similarly supportive to fix the issues, and we’ll have
   another discussion with them tomorrow to confirm if they can fix all issues
   by end-Jul

What is the outcome here?

   -

   The outcome is to optimise P0 seller UX issues so that we’ll be ready to
   onboard even more sellers starting next month
   -

   This is the full list of P0 issues inflicting sellers currently,
